weather.summary: Saturday: 10°C, light rain. Sunday: 10°C, overcast clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend offers a delightful mix of indoor and outdoor activities perfect for a 3-year-old in Hackney! From free museum visits and crafty Easter celebrations to exploring city farms and enjoying theatrical performances, there's something for every little explorer and creative mind. I've included many free options, and made sure to account for the chance of rain on Saturday.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: A library club with a reading corner and diverse book pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: It's free, drop-in, and offers a relaxed environment for little ones to explore books. Suitable for all ages up to 13 so you can come back as they grow.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Downs, then short walk
transport.tip: Check term times before you go!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: A museum designed specifically for children, with hands-on activities and exhibitions to encourage imagination, play, and design.
activities.whyItWorks: It's free, local, and filled with interactive exhibits perfect for a 3-year-old's curious mind. Mini Museum caters to under 2s with sensory experiences.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green, then short walk
transport.tip: Check out Sound Explorers and Mini Play sessions for babies and toddlers.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play spaces known as Story Worlds, plus a Story Garden playground. Baby Storytelling, crafts, and singalongs are also available.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive play areas are perfect for toddlers. The Story Garden is great for letting off steam.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Stratford, then short walk
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as it can get busy

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm Easter Activities
activities.description: Free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts. A variety of animals, including goats, sheep, and smaller animals.
activities.whyItWorks: It's free, involves animals and craft activities, and is easily accessible.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Vauxhall
transport.tip: A must for animal-lovers.

activities.title: Easter Family Community Celebration
activities.description: A free Easter family event with crafts, face painting, games, photo booth, chill-out zone, and children's entertainment.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of engaging activities for young children. A community-focused and welcoming event.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Woodford
transport.tip: No need to have attended church before, everyone is welcome.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore the concept of 'home' through different eras and perspectives.
activities.whyItWorks: It's free, local, and offers a look at how homes have changed which can spark a child's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hoxton, then short walk
transport.tip: Check for family-friendly events on their website

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for toddlers, offering a safe and engaging space for play.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Stratford
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the nearby Discover Children's Story Centre

activities.title: Visit a City Farm
activities.description: See spring arrivals and learn about farm animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals are always a hit with toddlers, and it's a chance to get outdoors.

transport.fromHackney: Various, depending on the farm. Check local listings.
transport.tip: Check opening times and any special events beforehand

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating exhibits, and family-friendly activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs are a classic toddler favourite, and the museum is free to enter. The Easter Family Festival runs from April 5-20.

transport.fromHackney: Piccadilly Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Book a free timed entry ticket in advance to avoid queues.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works through interactive exhibits, including a space exploration gallery and the Wonderlab (paid entry).
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for younger children to discover construction, water, light, and sound. Free entry.

transport.fromHackney: Piccadilly Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: The Wonderlab offers a great hands-on experience for a small fee.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world cultures and history, with highlights including the Egyptian galleries and mummies.
activities.whyItWorks: The Egyptian galleries are captivating for young children. Activity backpacks are available.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Holborn or Tottenham Court Road
transport.tip: Book a free timed entry ticket in advance to avoid queues.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: A family-friendly museum with natural history, musical instruments, and anthropology exhibits. Includes gardens and a small animal farm.
activities.whyItWorks: The gardens and animal farm provide outdoor space and animal encounters. The museum is manageable for little children and less crowded than larger museums.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill
transport.tip: Check out the Butterfly House and Aquarium for a small fee.

activities.title: Cutty Sark
activities.description: Explore a historic sailing ship and learn about its voyages.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for imaginative play and exploring a real ship.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark
transport.tip: Check for family events on their website.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Discover stories of sea travel, exploration, trade, and naval history. The AHOY! Children's gallery is an immersive soft play space.
activities.whyItWorks: The AHOY! gallery is perfect for young children. Free entry.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark, then short walk
transport.tip: Check out Play Tuesdays for under 5s.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: A riverside hub of arts, fun, and food with kids' concerts, festivals, creative workshops, and open spaces.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a wide range of activities, many of which are free. Plenty of space to move and explore.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Liverpool Street, then tube to Waterloo
transport.tip: Check the events calendar before heading out to plan your day.

activities.title: Mini Makers: Messy Play
activities.description: Messy play sessions for young children
activities.whyItWorks: Great for sensory exploration and creative fun.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to nearest station, check travel advice
transport.tip: Wear clothes you don't mind getting messy!

activities.title: Family Easter Makes Workshop at Kenwood
activities.description: Easter-themed family workshop.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative and festive activity for families.

transport.fromHackney: Check travel advice closer to the time
transport.tip: Book in advance as spaces are limited.

activities.title: Easter Trail
activities.description: Easter trail for families.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor activity that encourages exploration and problem-solving.

transport.fromHackney: Check travel advice closer to the time
transport.tip: Wear appropriate clothing for the weather.

activities.title: Paddington The Musical
activities.description: A musical adaptation of the beloved Paddington Bear stories.
activities.whyItWorks: Paddington is a well-loved character, and the musical format is engaging for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Charing Cross or Covent Garden
transport.tip: Consider a matinee performance for younger children.

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: A spectacular musical with stunning puppetry and live music.
activities.whyItWorks: Visually stunning and features familiar songs. A classic family show.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Covent Garden or Temple
transport.tip: Book seats closer to the front for a better view.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Hop through the palace gardens to find Lindt Gold Bunny statues and win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a fun treasure hunt and a chocolate reward.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance, especially during peak times.

activities.title: Tower Bridge Easter Family Activities
activities.dates: Tuesday 31 March, Tuesday 7 April and Saturday 11 April 2026
activities.description: Making, movement and mechanical marvels, giving children the chance to discover how Victorian engineers and inventors shaped the modern world.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers drop-in activities that combine making, movement and mechanical marvels, giving children the chance to discover how Victorian engineers and inventors shaped the modern world

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to London Bridge station, or Tower Hill Station.
transport.tip: Activities are drop-in, but it's best to arrive earlier in the day.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Bluey at Kew
activities.description: Bluey-themed interactive activities for the whole family, including a Play Zone with 3D installations and the chance to meet the Bluey character.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers Bluey-themed interactive activities for the whole family, including a Play Zone with 3D installations and the chance to meet the Bluey character

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially during peak times.
